summary: Linked `src/melee/gr/grzebesroute.c` by changing it from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`. The matching work mainly replaced manually named local constants and strings with compiler-friendly local literals, `__FILE__`, `HSD_ASSERT`, and small inline helper wrappers around light-object accessors/field reads. Symbol metadata was updated so former global `grZe_Route_*` constants/strings became local labels like `@211`, `@270`, and local section labels, reflecting that the C now emits them as local data rather than exported globals.
what_changed: Several explicit global/static data definitions were removed from C: the file-name string `grZe_Route_803E5E64`, sdata assertion strings `grZe_Route_804D4840` and `grZe_Route_804D4848`, and multiple `sdata2` float/GXColor constants from `grZe_Route_804DB918` through `grZe_Route_804DB944`.
`OSReport` now passes `__FILE__` instead of the manually declared `grZe_Route_803E5E64` file-name string.
The clamp and camera-position code in `grZebesRoute_8020B42C` now uses direct literals `-50.0f` and `0.0f` instead of named sdata2 globals.

`grZebesRoute_8020B548` was converted from a nonmatching stub-commented function into a matching implementation using direct float literals, local `GXColor` initializers, `HSD_ASSERT`, and inline helper functions.

The light update loop in `grZebesRoute_8020B548` was rewritten as a `for` loop advancing through `HSD_LObjGetNext`, handling spot lights and upper/lower point lights with scaled positions and distance attenuation.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` was adjusted to mark many previously global `grZe_Route_*` constants/strings as local labels such as `@187`, `@211`, `@270`, `@274`, and `@278`, and to add local labels `...rodata.0` and `...data.0` at existing route data addresses.
smart_moves: Using `__FILE__` and `HSD_ASSERT` let the compiler generate local file/assertion strings instead of requiring explicit globally named string objects.

Inlining small wrappers around `light_gobj->hsd_obj`, `HSD_LObjGetNext`, and `lobj->flags & LOBJ_TYPE_MASK` appears to have been used as a targeted codegen-shaping tactic for the formerly nonmatching lighting routine.
Replacing named constants with immediate float literals and local `GXColor` initializers reduced unnecessary global symbol pressure and aligned emitted local data with the linker map.

Updating `symbols.txt` scopes at the same time as the C changes kept the configuration consistent with the new object output, especially for local compiler-emitted constants in `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2`.

The implementation preserves stage-lighting behavior while reshaping source expression form: spot light color/position/interest, upper point light, and lower point light handling remain explicit and readable.
decomp_lessons: For matching work, do not automatically preserve every discovered `grZe_Route_*` data symbol as a named global; if the compiler can emit it as a local literal or local aggregate, update symbol scope accordingly.
`__FILE__` can be preferable to a manually declared file-name string when matching assert/report callsites that use source-file metadata.
`HSD_ASSERT(line, expr)` may better match original debug/assert codegen than hand-written `if` plus `__assert(file, line, string)` patterns.
Small inline helper functions can be useful for forcing desired loads/calls around HSD object access without changing observable behavior.
Local aggregate initialization of `GXColor` inside branches can match better than referencing separate global `const GXColor` objects.
When linking a stage route file, expect paired changes in `configure.py` and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`; codegen fixes may require symbol visibility/local-label cleanup, not just C edits.
naming_conventions: Stage route functions in this file use the `grZebesRoute_8020Bxxx` naming pattern.
Route data symbols use the stage prefix `grZe_Route_` followed by an address, for example `grZe_Route_804D6A60`.
Compiler/local labels in `symbols.txt` are represented with names like `@187`, `@211`, `@270`, and section-local labels like `...rodata.0` and `...data.0`.

Inline helpers added for matching were named after the containing function with a numeric suffix: `grZebesRoute_8020B548_inline1`, `grZebesRoute_8020B548_inline2`, and `grZebesRoute_8020B548_inline3`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed object status from NonMatching to Matching only after code and symbol-scope updates.
Converted global float constants such as `-50.0F`, `0.0F`, `600.0F`, `1000.0F`, `400.0F`, `45.0F`, `0.99F`, `500.0F`, and `0.03F` into direct literals at use sites.
Converted global color constants into local `GXColor color = { ... }` initializers inside the relevant light-type branches.
Used a `for` loop with update expression `i++, lobj = grZebesRoute_8020B548_inline2(lobj)` instead of a `while` loop with manual increment/update at the bottom.
Used inline wrappers to influence expression lowering for HSD light object retrieval, next-object traversal, and light-type flag masking.
Replaced explicit `__assert(grZe_Route_803E5E64, line, string)` calls with `HSD_ASSERT(line, expr)` forms.
Adjusted `symbols.txt` from global `grZe_Route_*` entries to local labels where the matching object emits local constants rather than externally visible symbols.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was merged quickly after creation; the provided context includes no human discussion explaining reviewer rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find other stage route files still marked NonMatching near `grzebesroute.c` in `configure.py` that may benefit from the same local literal and assert cleanup tactics.
Search for manually declared file-name strings passed to `OSReport` or `__assert` that could be replaced by `__FILE__` or `HSD_ASSERT` for matching.
Search for global `GXColor` or float constants in route files that should become local literals/local labels during matching.
Compare `grkinokoroute.c`, `grshrineroute.c`, and `grbigblueroute.c` for similar HSD light traversal patterns and possible inline-helper codegen tactics.
